sections.paragraph: (“the back bone of a man”), [Ezion-geber], pr.n. of a maritime city in Idumæa, situated on the Ælanitic gulf of the Red Sea, not far from Elath (see אֵילַת ); whence Solomon’s fleet sailed to Ophir. Called by the Greeks Berenice; see Jos. Antt. viii. 6, § 4. In the time of the Arab dominion عصيون: Num 33:35 Deu 2:8 1Ki 9:26 22:49 Burckhardt’s Travels in Syria, Germ. ed. p. 831.
